skipped.reason: False positive: repo already uses PyJWT 2.12.0 (fix threshold is 2.4.0), pyproject.toml constrains >=2.4.0, and all jwt.decode() calls explicitly pass the algorithms argument

structured_output.reasoning: CVE-2022-29217 affects PyJWT before 2.4.0, allowing algorithm confusion when jwt.decode() is called without an explicit algorithms argument. This is a false positive because: (1) The repo pins pyjwt==2.12.0 in requirements/base.txt, well above the 2.4.0 fix threshold. (2) pyproject.toml specifies PyJWT>=2.4.0, preventing installation of vulnerable versions. (3) All PyJWT jwt.decode() calls in source code (oauth2.py, async_query_manager.py) and tests already explicitly pass the algorithms argument. (4) The one jwt.decode() call without algorithms (jwt_verifier.py:568) uses authlib.jose, not PyJWT — confirmed by authlib.jose.errors imports. Posted detailed analysis as a comment on the source issue.
